title: Your First English Self-Introduction
learningGoal: The learner will successfully construct at least 5 grammatically accurate English sentences in the Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) order to introduce themselves, state their country, and properly conclude the message.
instructions: Now it is your turn to write! Imagine you are meeting a new English pen-pal on the internet. Write a short paragraph introducing yourself. You must say hello, state your name, state where you are from, mention how you are feeling, and say goodbye. You must write at least 80 words. Use the Word Bank for help with spelling.

contentItems.text: Do not translate Hindi SOV directly to English. Ensure the verb comes after the subject!

writingPrompt: Write a self-introduction paragraph meeting a new pen-pal. Greet them, state your name, your country, your mood today, and politely say goodbye.
promptGoal: Demonstrate understanding of basic greeting spelling, SVO sentence structure, and capitalization rules.
referenceHints: Always start a sentence with a Capital Letter.
End your sentences with a full stop (period) '.'

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: Hello! Good morning. My name is Rohan. I am from India. I am a student. Today, I am very fine and happy to meet you. Goodbye! Take care.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: हेलो! गुड मॉर्निंग। माई नेम इज़ रोहन। आई एम फ्रॉम इंडिया। आई एम अ स्टूडेंट। टुडे, आई एम वेरी फाइन एंड हैप्पी टू मीट यू। गुडबाय! टेक केयर।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: नमस्ते! शुभ प्रभात। मेरा नाम रोहन है। मैं भारत से हूँ। मैं एक छात्र हूँ। आज, मैं बहुत ठीक हूँ और आपसे मिलकर खुश हूँ। अलविदा! अपना ख्याल रखना।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: This is an excellent answer because it perfectly follows SVO structure, uses capitalization perfectly, and fulfills every requirement of the prompt.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: Hi. I am Priya. I am from India. I am fine. Goodbye.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: हाय। आई एम प्रिया। आई एम फ्रॉम इंडिया। आई एम फाइन। गुडबाय।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: नमस्ते। मैं प्रिया हूँ। मैं भारत से हूँ। मैं ठीक हूँ। अलविदा।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: This is acceptable because it meets the minimum requirements, but the sentences are very basic and short. It could be expanded for a higher score.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner put the verb in the correct position? Did they avoid translating Hindi SOV directly?
Are names capitalized? Is 'I' capitalized? Do sentences end with full stops?
Did they correctly spell and use greetings, state their name/country, and correctly say goodbye?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Hello! Good morning. My name is Rohan. I am from India. I am a student. Today, I am very fine and happy to meet you. Goodbye! Take care.
acceptableAlternates: Hi, my name is Amit. I live in India. I am happy today. See you later.
Good afternoon. I am Priya. I am from Delhi. I am fine thank you. Bye.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Subject + Verb + Object structures throughout. First person pronouns used properly.

feedbackRules.onPass: Wonderful! You have written your first proper English introduction. You used SVO grammar correctly and capitalized your 'I's.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please check your capitalization. Remember 'I' must always be capital, and verbs come right after the subject. Rewrite and try again.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good! However, you may have missed some punctuation rules or used a slightly awkward translated sentence. Review the Model Output and try once more.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Write a self-introduction paragraph meeting a new pen-pal.

hintText: Write your thoughts in short chunks. Subject -> Verb -> Object. Example: I (subject) am (verb) fine (object).
commonMistakes: Writing 'I name is Rahul' instead of 'My name is Rahul'.
Forgetting to put a space after periods.
